content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
November 13,
1996
Lydia Shayne
Executive Director
Lydia Shane Communications
829 N.
Highland Avenue
Los Angeles,
CA
90038
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A-96-294
Dear Ms.
Shayne:
This is in response to your request for advice on behalf of
Los Angeles City Councilmember Michael Feuer regarding the
campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the “Act”).
1
QUESTION
Would a contribution to Councilman Michael Feuer result from
the inclusion of the words “Special Thanks to Councilman Michael
Feuer”
on an emergency preparedness booklet and door hangers
advertising an emergency preparedness program?
CONCLUSION
No.
A contribution to Councilman Feuer will not result.
FACTS
A private nonprofit organization, the Jewish Family Service
(“JFS”),
is sponsoring an emergency preparedness pilot program
called Safety NET.
JFS will prepare a series of five door hangers
using state and federal earthquake funds administered through the
city’s Community Development Department.
According to our
telephone conversations, city funds are not used to finance the
program.
1
Government Code Sections 81000—91015.
Commission regulations
appear at Title 2,
Sections 18000-18995 of the California Code of
Regulations.
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


File No.
A—96—294
Page
2
You stated in a telephone conversation with Lynda Doherty of
our staff that the series of door hangers will advertise free
emergency preparedness seminars.
Safety NET would like to include
the words “Special Thanks to Councilman Michael Feuer”
on the door
hangers.
In addition,
Safety NET will print 25,000 copies of an
emergency preparedness booklet to distribute to city residents.
The booklet will be a compilation of the information on the five
door hangers, the information provided in the free seminars and
additional emergency preparedness information.
Safety NET would
like to include the words “Special Thanks to Michael Feuer”
on the
back cover of the booklet.
ANALYSIS
Section 82015 defines “contribution” as any payment,
forgiveness of a loan,
a payment of a loan by a third party,
or an
enforceable promise to make a payment except to the extent that
full and adequate consideration is received unless it is clear
from the surrounding circumstances that it is not made for
political purposes.
A payment is made for political purposes if
it is:
(1)
for the purpose of influencing or attempting to
influence the action of the voters for or against the nomination
or election of a candidate or candidates,
or the qualification or
passage of any measure;
(2)
received by or made at the behest
of a candidate.
2
(Regulation 18215(a).)
Section 82007 defines “candidate,” in pertinent part,
as any
individual who is listed on the ballot for elective office.
An
individual who becomes a candidate shall retain his or her status
as a candidate until such time as that status is terminated
pursuant to Section 84214.
Thus,
as an elected official,
a city
councilmember is considered a candidate under Section 82007.
Consequently,
any payments a city councilmember behests are
presuiuedto be for political purposes and will be considered
2
Regulation 18215 provides:
“A payment is made for political
purposes if it is:
(1)
for the purpose of influencing or
attempting to influence the action of the voters for or against
the nomination or election of a candidate or candidates,
or the
qualification or passage of any measure; or
(2)
received by or
made at the behest of the following or any agent thereof:
(A)
a
candidate;
(B)
a controlled committee;
(C)
an official committee
of a political party,
including a state central committee, county
central committee,
assembly district committee or any subcommittee
of such committee; or
(D)
an organization formed or existing
primarily for political purposes,
including,
but not limited to,
a
political action committee established by any membership
organization,
labor union or corporation.


File No.
A—96—294
Page
3
contributions absent an exception.
(Danner Advice Letter,
A—96—
039. )
Regulation 18215
(c) provides,
in pertinent part, that
contribution does not include:
(4)
A payment made at the behest of a
candidate,
which is for a communication by the
candidate or any other person, that meets all of
the following:
(i)
Does not contain express advocacy;
(ii)
Does not make reference to the
candidate’s candidacy for elective office, the
candidate’s election campaign,
or the candidate’s
or his or her opponent’s qualifications for office;
and
(iii)
Does not solicit contributions to the
candidate or to third persons for use in support of
the candidate or in opposition to the candidate’s
opponent.
You will be including the words “Special Thanks to Councilman
Michael Feuer” at the top of a series of door hangers.
The door
hangers you describe are communications intended to communicate
the facts about the Safety NET program.
Further,
Safety NET will
produce emergency preparedness booklets.
The booklets are printed
material containing information from emergency preparedness
seminars and additional emergency preparedness facts.
According
to your facts,
none of these materials contain express advocacy,
they do not make reference to Councilman Feuer’s candidacy for
elective office or his qualifications for office,
and they do not
solicit contributions.
The only reference to Councilman Feuer is
the thank you message.
Therefore, the door hangers and the
booklets fit within the exception set forth in Regulation
18215(c) (4)
and are not contributions to Councilman Feuer.
A candidate “behests” a contribution when the contribution is
made “under the control or at the direction of,
in cooperation,
consultation, coordination,
or concert with, at the request or
suggestion of,
or with the express, prior consent of.”
(Regulation 18225.7(a).)


File No.
A—96—294
Page
4
If you have any further questions, please feel free to
contact me at
(916)
322—5660.
SGC:LR:ak
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
Counsel,
Legal Division

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Would a contribution to Councilman Michael Feuer result from
the inclusion of the words “Special Thanks to Councilman Michael
Feuer”
on an emergency preparedness booklet and door hangers
advertising an emergency preparedness program?

CONCLUSION: No.
A contribution to Councilman Feuer will not result.
